nodes.label: Greetings & Set Phrases
nodes.description: Master essential daily greetings, name introductions, and departures.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask names: **Wie heißen Sie?** / **Wie heißt du?**. Leaving/returning phrases: **Ich gehe jetzt** (*I'm leaving now*) or **Auf Wiedersehen**. Home arrivals: **Ich bin wieder da** (*I'm back*) and **Willkommen zurück** (*Welcome back*).

nodes.label: Countries & Identity
nodes.description: Talk about nationalities, languages, and core family relationships.
nodes.cheatsheet: Nationalities append suffix variations: **Ich bin Deutscher** (m) / **Deutsche** (f). Languages use standalone nouns: **Ich spreche Deutsch**. For 'Mom', use **Mama** or the formal **Mutter**.

nodes.label: Numbers, Hundreds & Thousands
nodes.description: Count higher figures into hundreds and thousands, and learn basic fractions.
nodes.cheatsheet: Hundreds: **einhundert** (100), **zweihundert** (200). Thousands: **eintausend** (1000). Fractions combine cardinal numbers and a special suffix: 2/3 is **zwei Drittel**.

nodes.label: Demonstratives (This, That, Which)
nodes.description: Identify objects using gender-coded demonstrative articles.
nodes.cheatsheet: Demonstratives decline by case and gender: **dieser** (m), **diese** (f), **dieses** (n) for 'this'. 'That' uses stressed articles: **der**, **die**, **das**. 'Which' is **welcher/welche/welches**.

nodes.label: Colours & Descriptions
nodes.description: Use basic color adjectives to describe objects.
nodes.cheatsheet: Colors are lowercase adjectives: **rot** (red), **blau** (blue), **schwarz** (black), **weiß** (white), **grün** (green). If used before nouns, they require adjective endings (e.g., *ein rotes Buch*).

nodes.label: General Counters
nodes.description: Count plural items accurately using structural plural endings.
nodes.cheatsheet: German does not use counter words, but changes the noun plural form (e.g., *ein Buch*, *zwei Bücher*). Ask 'How many?' using **Wie viele?**.

nodes.label: Floors & Negative Numbers
nodes.description: Navigate building levels, floors, basements, and negative figures.
nodes.cheatsheet: Count floors using ordinals: **erster Stock** (1st floor). Ground floor is **Erdgeschoss**. Basement is **Untergeschoss** or **Keller**. Negative values/temperatures use **minus** (*minus fünf*).

nodes.label: Buildings & Prepositions
nodes.description: Identify common spatial structures and use two-way prepositions.
nodes.cheatsheet: Structures: **das Gebäude** (building), **der Bahnhof** (station). Prepositions are two-way (Dative for location): **auf** (on top), **unter** (under), **vor** (in front), **hinter** (behind). E.g., *vor dem Bahnhof*.

nodes.label: Existence & Amount of People
nodes.description: Differentiate spatial existence and express amounts of people.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use the existential idiom **es gibt** (+Accusative) for general existence, or **ist/sind** for specific location. Count people normally with regular numbers: **zwei Personen** (two people).

nodes.label: Relative Time: Day, Week, Month, Year
nodes.description: Anchor timelines cleanly across relative days, weeks, months, and years.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Timeline grid:
Days: **vorgestern** → **gestern** → **heute** → **morgen** → **übermorgen**.
Weeks: **letzte Woche** → **diese Woche** → **nächste Woche**.
Months: **letzten Monat** → **diesen Monat** → **nächsten Monat**.
Years: **letztes Jahr** → **dieses Jahr** → **nächstes Jahr**.

nodes.label: Specific Time: This Morning & Next Week
nodes.description: Isolate immediate parts of a day or adjacent calendar blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: Isolate times: **heute Morgen** (*this morning*) — do not confuse with *morgen* (tomorrow). 'Next week' is **nächste Woche**.

nodes.label: Months & Days of the Month
nodes.description: Identify calendar months and navigate dative ordinal calendar dates.
nodes.cheatsheet: Months: **Januar**, **Februar**, etc. Dates use dative ordinal suffixes after **am**: 'on May 1st' is **am ersten Mai**; 'on the 4th' is **am vierten**.

nodes.label: Year & Month Durations
nodes.description: Calculate spans and durations of time using years and month blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: Express time lengths with **Monat(e)** or **Jahr(e)** following the preposition **für** (+Accusative) or **seit** (+Dative): e.g., *für zwei Monate* (for two months).

nodes.label: Asking 'When' (Wann)
nodes.description: Formulate open-ended temporal questions about future, present, or past events.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use the question word **Wann?** (*When?*). Example: **Wann fliegst du nach Deutschland?** (*When are you flying to Germany?*).

nodes.label: Meals & Food Types
nodes.description: Categorize daily meals and basic dish selections.
nodes.cheatsheet: Meals: **das Frühstück** (breakfast), **das Mittagessen** (lunch), **das Abendessen** (dinner). Food item example: **Sushi**.

nodes.label: Consumptive Actions (Eat & Drink)
nodes.description: Utilize active consumption roots for eating and drinking.
nodes.cheatsheet: Verbs: **essen** (to eat - irregular change: *du isst*) and **trinken** (to drink). Direct objects drop into the Accusative case: **Ich trinke einen Tee** (m. Accusative).

nodes.label: Verb Conjugation & Tenses
nodes.description: Conjugate polite actions into affirmative, negative, past, and modal desire states.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Structural examples using **essen**:
Affirmative: **Ich esse** (*I eat*)
Negative: **Ich esse kein Sushi** (noun negation via *kein*)
Past: Conversational past uses *haben* + past participle: **Ich habe Sushi gegessen**
Desire: Modal verb **möchte** + infinitive at the end: **Ich möchte Sushi essen**. Question: **Möchtest du Sushi essen?**.

nodes.label: Conjunctions (Und auch & Danach)
nodes.description: Link phrases together or order successive events smoothly.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use **Und auch / Sowie** (*And also / Plus*) to add arguments. Use **Danach / Dann** (*And then / After that*) to sequence events—triggers inversion: *Danach esse ich*.

nodes.label: Frequency of Verbs
nodes.description: Quantify how often routines occur using positive and negative adverbs.
nodes.cheatsheet: Scale routines: **oft** (often), **manchmal** (sometimes), **selten** (rarely), and **nie** (never). Example: **Ich esse nie Fleisch** (*I never eat meat*).

nodes.label: Transportation & Commuting
nodes.description: Describe modes of travel and ask others how they commute.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask 'How do you go to work?' with **Wie kommst du zur Arbeit?**. Mark vehicles with preposition **mit** (+Dative): **mit dem Flugzeug** (by plane), **mit dem Auto** (by car), **mit dem Schiff** (by boat). Walking uses the idiom: **zu Fuß**.

nodes.label: Sports & Complex Preferences
nodes.description: Express preferences regarding sports, distinguishing what vs. what kind.
nodes.cheatsheet: To ask 'What sports...?' use **Welchen Sport magst du?**. For 'What kind of sports...?' use **Was für Sport magst du?**. Express love for an activity by adding the adverb **gern**: **Ich spiele gern Fußball**.
